summary: Backend testing complete for iteration 3 (customizable alert rules). 49/49 tests passed (21 new iteration-3 tests + 28 iteration-1/2 regression tests, 100%). Coverage: GET /api/alert-rules (empty + sorted desc by created_at), POST with all 3 valid types (forbidden_online, expected_online with grace_minutes, inactivity with max_silence_hours), POST validation (invalid type -> 400 with helpful list of valid types, invalid phone -> 400, days_of_week sanitization drops 9 -> [0,3], start_hour=30 -> clamped to 23), PATCH (enabled=false, multi-field name+days_of_week+grace_minutes, empty body -> 400, nonexistent rule -> 404), DELETE (returns {ok:true} + 404 on second delete). Trigger logic was verified by directly importing _evaluate_rule and _trigger_alert from server.py and invoking them with seeded MongoDB data: forbidden_online fires when monitor.status=online and now is in window, alert event is inserted with detail '[<rule_name>] <message>', last_triggered_at is updated, ALERT_COOLDOWN_SECONDS=3600 suppresses immediate retrigger, and re-fires after cooldown expires; out-of-window evaluation does not fire; inactivity rule fires when last online event > max_silence_hours ago. Regression smoke (whatsapp/status, monitors, events, settings) all green.

minor.issue: end_hour is clamped to max 24 but minimum allowed is 0; semantics of start_hour > end_hour (window wraps midnight) work in _in_window but are not documented at the API surface. Consider rejecting end_hour==start_hour (zero-width window) or documenting the wrap behavior.
When `name` is a whitespace-only string the code falls back to `Alert <phone>` via `payload.name.strip() or f'Alert {phone}'` — but this is silent. A 400 'name is required' would be cleaner.

No clamping is applied on PATCH (start_hour/end_hour/grace_minutes/max_silence_hours) — a user can PATCH start_hour=99 and it persists raw. Create-time validation is enforced but update-time is not. Mirror the clamping rules from POST.

window_start is computed via `now.replace(hour=start_hour, ...)` which silently breaks for windows that wrap midnight (start_hour>end_hour) — for those rules `window_start` is set to *today* at the late hour, so before the wrap point the offline duration is negative. Consider handling the wrap case explicitly.

Alert email send is not gated by an alert-specific flag — it reuses settings.email_enabled (the same flag that controls per-presence-change emails). Users who want presence emails but no alert spam (or vice-versa) cannot configure that. Consider a separate alert_email_enabled.

Catches Exception inside the rule loop but on outer error sleeps another 60s after the initial 60s sleep — the first sleep at top of try is the only pacing; a tight error path would loop fast. Same pattern as daily_summary_loop flagged in iteration 2.

action_items: Optional: enforce clamping & validation on PATCH /api/alert-rules/{id} for start_hour/end_hour/grace_minutes/max_silence_hours to mirror POST.
Optional: handle midnight-wrap windows in _evaluate_rule (expected_online) so window_start is computed correctly when start_hour > end_hour.
Optional: add a dedicated `alert_email_enabled` setting separate from `email_enabled` (presence emails).
Carryover: split server.py (~990 lines now) into routers/ + services/ modules — alerts, daily summary, internal webhook, models.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py grew from ~700 to ~990 lines after iteration 3 — split into modules (routers/alert_rules.py, services/alerts.py, services/scheduler.py, models.py).

_evaluate_rule mixes rule-validation, DB lookups, and message formatting; extract per-type evaluators (evaluate_forbidden_online / evaluate_expected_online / evaluate_inactivity) for unit testability.
PATCH endpoint accepts unbounded integers (no clamp) — easy bypass of POST validation.
ALERT_TYPES, ALERT_COOLDOWN_SECONDS hardcoded — promote to env vars or settings.
_trigger_alert inserts the event and broadcasts BEFORE updating last_triggered_at; if the update fails the alert can fire repeatedly. Consider transaction or update-first semantics.
alert_evaluator_loop loads ALL monitors (to_list(500)) every minute even when only a few rules exist — fine at this scale but fetch only monitors for phones referenced by enabled rules.
seed_data_creation: Tests seed data directly into MongoDB via motor (test phones 33699887766, 33655443322, 33644445555). Trigger test seeds a monitor with status='online' and a forbidden_online rule covering all weekdays/hours, then directly invokes server._evaluate_rule + server._trigger_alert (rebinding server.db to a client bound to the per-test asyncio loop to avoid 'Event loop is closed'). Cleanup deletes all test rules/monitors/events before & after the run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: WA client still in 'qr' state. To exercise alert trigger flow without the 60s background loop, import server module and call server._evaluate_rule(rule, monitor, now) and server._trigger_alert(rule, msg, now). IMPORTANT: rebind `server.db = AsyncIOMotorClient(MONGO_URL)[DB_NAME]` inside each test's asyncio.run, otherwise motor's lazy IO loop binding triggers 'Event loop is closed' across tests. Iteration 3 added /api/alert-rules CRUD + alert_evaluator_loop + _evaluate_rule + _trigger_alert + ALERT_COOLDOWN_SECONDS=3600 + ALERT_TYPES={forbidden_online, expected_online, inactivity}. Email-on-alert path reuses settings.email_enabled + email_recipient + RESEND_API_KEY (verified by code path; not hard-tested via Resend in this iteration since it is fire-and-forget).
